matches.x: mies, : To the chief Musician, Al-taschith, Michtam of David; when Saul sent, and they watched the house to kill

mies, : To the chief Musician, Al-taschith, Michtam of David; when Saul sent, and they watched the house to kill

And the whole earth was of one language (Gen. 11:1). Scripture states elsewhere in allusion to this verse: Slay them not, lest my people forget, make them wander to and fro by Thy power, and bring them down, O Lord our shield. For the sin of their mouth, and the words of their lips (Ps. 59:12). David uttered this verse in reference to Doeg and Ahithophel.29Doeg informed against the priests in Nob…

Another explanation of this verse. David said this in reference to the generation of the separation. Slay them not, lest my people forget that You have destroyed the generation of the flood. Rather, make them wander to and fro by Thy power, that is, scatter them throughout the world, as it is written: The Lord dispersed them from there (Gen. 11:8). Bring them down, because they said: Let us build…

Chapter 32: Prophets [1] These are the nations that the Lord left to test Israel with (Judges 3). It is written, "Do not kill them, lest my people forget; make them totter by your power and bring them down, O Lord our shield" (Psalms 59:12). Since the time that the Lord was revealed to Moses, He was seen only through the hand of an angel, as it is written, "And the angel of the Lord appeared to…

“The entire earth was of one language and of common speech” (Genesis 11:1). “The entire earth was of one language…” – Rabbi Elazar in the name of Rabbi Yosei bar Zimra began: “Do not kill them, lest my people forget; scatter them by Your power and take them down” (Psalms 59:12). The Rabbis interpreted the verse regarding Do’eg and Aḥitofel. David said [to God]: ‘Do not kill Do’eg and Aḥitofel,…
